place: Woodbury city, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Woodbury was founded in 1683 by the Wood family from Bury, England, after Henry Wood escaped religious persecution at age 80.
The Woodbury Friends Meeting House, built in 1715 and expanded in 1785, stands as one of the town’s oldest religious structures.
Rail service returned to Woodbury in 1854 when the West Jersey Railroad restored tracks, linking the town to the rest of South Jersey.
At the dawn of the twentieth century, Woodbury’s glass industry thrived, with the Woodbury Bottle Works producing a significant portion of the state’s glass.
Margaret Regional School, a Pre‑K to 8 Catholic elementary school, opened its doors in 1963 under the Diocese of Camden.
Woodbury Heights became an independent borough on April 6, 1915, after separating from Deptford Township following a local referendum.
